@@ -1,85 +1,78 @@
 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory
 
-## Current Status (Week 19 / Day 133 start)
-- **Cash: $7,177**
-- **Subscribers: 88 (all S1)**
-- **Open Issues: 48**
-- **Churn rate: ~6/week**
-- **0% lead conversion for 10+ weeks!**
+## Current Status (Week 23 / Day 161 start)
+- **Cash: $5,231**
+- **Subscribers: 67 (all S1)**
+- **Open Issues: 67**
+- **Churn rate: ~4-6/week**
+- **Net daily burn: ~$78/day**
 
-## ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS - Why No Conversion
-- Quality expectations rose above our delivered quality due to competitor events
-- S1 delivered quality at T3: 0.2274 (not enough for new leads at any price $7+)
-- 549 S1 leads lost with 0% conversion over weeks 9-18
-- Leads show promo=0 in database, suggesting lead_promotion may NOT affect evaluation
-- Lead promotion only reduces first billing (not evaluation quality-price check)
-- Dev spending is prohibitively expensive to close the quality gap
-- R&D is unaffordable ($166K minimum)
+## Week 23 Actions (NEW: Testing Lead Promotion)
+- Set lead_promotion for S1 = $6/mo (new leads see Plan A at effective $1)
+  - Rationale: At $1 effective price, quality 0.1879 should easily exceed requirements
+  - Goal: Start converting the ~8 organic leads/week that were being lost
+- Added $5/day ad spend on social_media for S1 (generating more leads)
+- Increased ads_strength from 0.3 to 0.4 (extra ~$1/day ad revenue)
+- Kept ongoing promo at S1 Plan A = $2 (not worth increasing)
+- Kept ops at $5/day (prevents outages)
+- Posted social media: "Investing in product quality improvements..."
 
-## Financial Analysis
-- Monthly revenue: $917/mo from 88 subs (avg ~$11/sub)
-- Daily revenue: ~$35 subs + $3.7 ads = $38.7/day
-- Capacity tier 0: $85/day (FIXED, cannot reduce)
-- Compute at T3: ~$10/day
-- Total costs minimum: ~$98-100/day
-- **Net burn: ~$60/day = ~$420/week**
-- **Runway: ~17 weeks (to ~week 36)**
-- **Game ends: week 71 (day 497)**
+## Financial Trajectory
+- Revenue: ~$32/day subs + ~$5/day ads = $37/day
+- Costs: $85 cap + ~$11 compute + $5 ops + $5 ads + $1 acquisition = ~$107/day
+- Net burn: ~$70/day ≈ $490/week
+- Projected bankruptcy: Week 33-35 (day 230-245)
+- Game ends: Week 71 (day 497)
 
-## Week 19 Configuration (SURVIVAL MODE)
-### Prices & Tiers
-- Prices: A=$7, B=$15, C=$79
-- Tiers: T3/T3/T3 (kept for quality retention)
-- Quotas: 300/1500/10000
+## Key Metric: Can Lead Promotion Convert?
+- PREVIOUS result: promo=0 in DB for lost leads (lead_promotion may NOT affect evaluation)
+- BUT the instruction doc says: "Lead promotions reduce the effective price new customers see, increasing conversion rates"
+- This is the CRITICAL test. If leads convert this week, we have a growth path.
+- If they don't convert (0 new subs again), then lead_promotion truly doesn't work and we're in pure milking mode.
 
-### Promotions
-- Ongoing: global=$0, S1 Plan A=$2 (effective A price=$5 for retention)
-- Lead promo: global=$5 (may help first-bill, doubtful on evaluation)
+## Quality Analysis (S1)
+- S1 Plan A T2: quality 0.1879 (with +0.03 group bonus)
+- S1 Plan B T3: quality 0.2255
+- S1 quality_floor: 0.093
+- S1 WTP: ~$40.72 (±25%)
+- At quality 0.1879, price must be ~$3 or less for new leads to convert (without tenure bonus)
+- Existing subs have tenure bonus (~+0.08 at 4 months) keeping them on plans
 
-### Ad Spend
-- ALL CUT TO $0 (was $6/day on social_media S1, completely wasted)
+## Quality for Other Groups
+- S2: floor=0.327, WTP=$155 → impossible for us (quality max 0.204)
+- S3: floor=0.185, WTP=$188 → Plan B barely at 0.1985, only at very low prices
+- E1: floor=0.591 → impossible
+- E2: floor=0.851 → impossible  
+- E3: floor=0.719 → impossible
 
-### Other Spend
-- Ops: $5/day (reduced from $10 - minimum to prevent disaster)
-- Dev: $0/day
-- Ads Strength: 0.3 (kept for $3.7/day ad revenue)
+## Subscription Breakdown (67 subs)
+- Plan A @ $3 eff=$1: 1 sub (listed=$3, promo=$2)
+- Plan A @ $7 eff=$5: 1 sub (listed=$7, promo=$2)
+- Plan A @ $9 eff=$7: 38 subs (listed=$9, promo=$2)
+- Plan A @ $19 eff=$17: 2 subs (listed=$19, promo=$2)
+- Plan B @ $15 eff=$15: 14 subs (no promo)
+- Plan B @ $19 eff=$19: 10 subs (no promo)
+- Plan B @ $25 eff=$25: 1 sub (no promo)
+- Total effective monthly: ~$731/mo
 
-### Social Media
-- Posted: "Big improvements rolling out this month..."
+## Why 0% Conversion for 14 Weeks
+- Competitor events permanently raised S1 quality expectations
+- At Plan A price $7, new leads need quality ~0.25+ but we deliver 0.1879
+- Only way to convert: reduce effective price (lead_promotion) or increase quality
+- Quality increase via dev spend: prohibitively expensive ($700K+ for +0.03)
+- R&D tier 1: $167K, unaffordable
 
-## Revenue Breakdown (88 S1 Subs)
-- Plan A @$5: 1 sub = $5/mo
-- Plan A @$7: 34 subs = $238/mo
-- Plan A @$9: 32 subs = $288/mo
-- Plan B @$15: 4 subs = $60/mo
-- Plan B @$17: 4 subs = $68/mo
-- Plan A @$19: 4 subs = $76/mo
-- Plan B @$19: 7 subs = $133/mo
-- Plan B @$24: 1 sub = $24/mo
-- Plan B @$25: 1 sub = $25/mo
-- Total: $917/mo = $30.6/day + $3.7 ad rev = $34.3/day
+## Lessons Learned
+1. Lead promotion = FIRST test of whether it affects evaluation (previous result: promo=0 in DB for lost leads suggests it may NOT work)
+2. Competitor events permanently raise expectations beyond our quality range
+3. Capacity tier 0 at $85/day is the dominant cost (85% of total)
+4. Tenure loyalty bonus (~+0.08 at 4 months) is what keeps existing subs from churning faster
+5. Plan B subs paying $15-25 have no promo → at higher churn risk
+6. Each sub contributes ~$0.24/day net positive after compute costs
+7. Ad revenue scales with subs count × ads_strength (currently ~$5/day at 0.4)
 
-## Key Lessons Learned
-1. Lead promotion does NOT affect evaluation (promo=0 in DB for all lost leads)
-2. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations beyond our reach
-3. Capacity tier 0 at $85/day is the dominant cost blocking profitability
-4. Need ~300+ subs at $7/mo just to break even on capacity alone
-5. S1 is the ONLY segment we can serve (others need much higher quality)
-6. Dev spending has extreme diminishing returns: +0.048 quality costs $14K+
-7. At current quality, NO price point enables lead conversion
-8. The only economic play is to milk existing subs and minimize burn
-
-## Strategy
-- **SURVIVAL MODE: Maximize final cash position**
-- No spending on growth (it doesn't work)
-- Minimize discretionary costs
-- Accept subscriber decline
-- Revenue will decrease as subs churn
-- Capacity cost ($85/day) stays regardless - this is the killer
-
-## Forecast Trajectory
-- Week 20: ~$6,700 cash
-- Week 23: ~$5,100 cash  
-- Week 31: ~$900 cash
-- Week 37: ~$0 (bankrupt)
-- Game end (week 71): $0 unless something changes
\ No newline at end of file
+## Strategy Priority
+1. **TEST lead_promotion** - If it works, we can grow and extend runway significantly
+2. **Monitor new sub count next week** - Even 2-3 new subs validates the approach
+3. **If lead_promotion fails again**: Pure milking mode, cut ad spend to $0, minimize all costs
+4. **If lead_promotion works**: Consider increasing ad spend and ongoing promotion to grow sustainably
\ No newline at end of file
